primaryConversions.businessMeaning: A quote request was submitted and confirmed. This is the clearest current lead-intent event for service demand.
caveats: Recent volume is still low before paid campaigns scale.
Lead quality should be reviewed before aggressive automated bidding.
primaryConversions.minimumRecommendedVolumeBeforeAggressive: At least 30-50 qualified conversions in a 30-day window per platform/campaign family.

primaryConversions.businessMeaning: A user completed account registration successfully.
primaryConversions.paidMediaRelevance: Primary acquisition conversion with quality caveats
caveats: Signup quality can vary if some accounts do not become shipping customers.
Should be interpreted with downstream activation data once available.
primaryConversions.minimumRecommendedVolumeBeforeAggressive: At least 30-50 qualified conversions in a 30-day window, plus review of activation quality.

secondaryConversions.businessMeaning: A contact form was submitted successfully.
secondaryConversions.whenUseful: Useful for measuring lead intent and support/commercial inquiry volume.
secondaryConversions.risks: May include support or general inquiries unless form purpose is clearly commercial.

secondaryConversions.businessMeaning: A calculator result was generated after a shipping-cost query.
secondaryConversions.whenUseful: Useful for remarketing, funnel diagnosis, and assessing product/service interest.
secondaryConversions.risks: Not a lead by itself; optimizing to it may favor price shoppers over qualified leads.

secondaryConversions.businessMeaning: A user clicked a WhatsApp contact CTA.
secondaryConversions.whenUseful: Useful as an intent signal and remarketing/audience signal.
secondaryConversions.risks: Click does not confirm a conversation or qualified lead.

secondaryConversions.businessMeaning: A user clicked a phone CTA.
secondaryConversions.whenUseful: Useful if phone calls become a campaign KPI.
secondaryConversions.risks: Click does not confirm a completed call; call tracking is needed before optimization.

secondaryConversions.businessMeaning: A user clicked an email CTA.
secondaryConversions.whenUseful: Useful for contact-intent reporting and UX analysis.
secondaryConversions.risks: Weaker than a confirmed form submission and does not confirm that an email was sent.

microConversions.businessMeaning: The user entered the quote funnel.
microConversions.diagnosticValue: Useful for funnel analysis, audience building, traffic-quality diagnosis, and early campaign learning.
microConversions.optimizationWarning: Do not use as a primary optimization goal; it can over-optimize toward shallow engagement.

quote_request_submit_success.recommendedInitialAction: Import as a primary conversion after Google Ads linking/import planning is approved.
quote_request_submit_success.caveats: Use as the main lead-generation optimization event; monitor quality and duplicate risk.

signup_success.recommendedInitialAction: Import only after confirming signup quality or start as secondary observation if quality is uncertain.
signup_success.caveats: Registration does not always equal qualified shipping customer.

contact_form_submit_success.recommendedInitialAction: Import as secondary observation only unless commercial lead purpose is confirmed.

calculator_result.recommendedInitialAction: Use for reporting, remarketing, and funnel diagnosis rather than primary bidding.
calculator_result.caveats: Can optimize toward calculators instead of leads if used as primary.

whatsapp_click.recommendedInitialAction: Track as secondary/off-site intent until conversation quality is measurable.
whatsapp_click.caveats: Click does not confirm completed WhatsApp conversation.

phone_click.recommendedInitialAction: Do not import for bidding until call tracking confirms completed and qualified calls.
phone_click.caveats: Click is not a completed call.

email_click.recommendedInitialAction: Track as a weak contact-intent signal only.
email_click.caveats: Click does not confirm message sent or lead quality.

quote_request_start.recommendedInitialAction: Keep in GA4 reporting and funnel analysis; do not use for bidding.

calculator_start.recommendedInitialAction: Keep in GA4 reporting unless a future approved phase defines a platform use.
calculator_start.caveats: Not approved as an ad-platform optimization event.

leadQualityLoopRecommendation.whyRawLeadsAreNotEnough: Raw leads can include curiosity, duplicates, support requests, spam, or low-quality registrations. Paid media should eventually optimize toward qualified and activated customers.
requirements: Conversion upload or offline conversion API design.
Stable IDs or platform click IDs handled without exposing PII in analytics artifacts.

privacyAndConsentConsiderations: No PII should be sent to GA4, GTM, Google Ads, or Meta analytics payloads.
Raw gclid/fbclid should not be exposed in client-side analytics payloads.
Only boolean flags such as gclid_present and fbclid_present are currently used.
Meta CAPI or offline conversion upload requires a separate privacy-safe design.
Consent Mode and Meta consent handling should be reviewed before implementation.

finalRecommendation: Use this conversion map as the approval artifact before connecting Google Ads.
Do not optimize toward diagnostic events.
Start with the smallest safe conversion set.
Keep secondary events as observation/reporting first.
